title: Formation Constants of the Complexes of 2-Hydroxy-1-Naphthaldoxime with Some Trivalent Metal Ions and UO22+
author: M. S. MAYADEO
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Ramnarain Ruia College, Bombay-400 019 

  Manuscript received 4 August 1977, revised 14 December 1977, accepted 10 April 1978  

 Potentiometric studies have been carried out on metal complexes of  \(UO_2^{2+}\) , Y 3+ , Pr 3+ , Gd 3+ , Dy 3+  and Nd 3+  with 2-hydroxy-1-napbthaldoxime. The proton-association constant of the reagent and the formation constants of its metal complexes have been determined by Bjerrum's method at 25°±0.1. The departure from the electrostatic bond between metal and donor atoms of the reagent has been attributed to the size of the hydrated metal ions of rare earths.

The departure from the electrostatic bond between metal and donor atoms of the reagent has been attributed to the size of the hydrated metal ions of rare earths.
